---
title: "Parsing to an AST"
subtitle: "T1 · Phase T1.2 · `va-frontend::parser`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: code complete, real coverage.** 59 tests, all green — recursive-descent, with a
precedence-climbing expression parser and support for module instantiation, `generate`/`genvar`
loops, vector nets, and 2-D array variables.
:::

## Goal

Turn the lexer's token stream into a surface AST: module structure, ports, ranged parameters,
the analog block's full control-flow set, and expressions — with correct operator precedence.

## Where it fits

```
 tokens ──► [parser] ──ModuleAst──► [elaborate] ──IR (Interface α)──► va-codegen
```

`parse` returns every `module...endmodule` in a source file, not just one — a real file can
define a subcircuit alongside a top module that instantiates it (`Item::Instance`, § module
instantiation below), and `va-frontend::elaborate_with_library` later resolves those references
against the whole set.

## The idea

**Recursive descent, one function per grammar production** — `Parser::parse_item` dispatches on
the next token/keyword to `parse_net_item`, `parse_param_item`, `parse_analog_block`, etc.
Nothing exotic; the interesting design decisions are elsewhere:

**Precedence climbing for expressions**, following the standard Verilog operator-precedence
table (IEEE 1364 Table 5-4) verbatim — `*`/`/` bind tighter than `+`/`-`, `**` (power) is
right-associative, the ternary is right-associative, and the full bitwise/shift family
(`& | ^ ^~ ~ << >>`) slots in at its own real precedence level rather than being bolted on
loosely.

**Module instantiation, resolved entirely in this crate.** `resistor r1(p, n);` or
`divider #(.gain(2.0)) d1(.in(a), .out(b));` parses into an `Item::Instance`; nothing about it
needs a `va-ir`/`va-codegen` change at all — `Elaborator::elaborate_with_library` recursively
inlines the referenced submodule's own arenas into the instantiating module before handoff, so
Interface α never had to grow a "one module references another" concept. Scalar and vector-slice
port connections (`CartesianMultiplier1(transfer, in[0:1], out[0:1]);`) both work; a
`generate`-driven *array* of instances does not (a stated v1 limit).

**Cross-file instantiation** works too, but at the `va-cli` layer, not here: this crate's own
`elaborate_with_library` already accepted an arbitrary `library: &[ModuleAst]` regardless of
which file an entry came from — `va-cli::check_models` groups files by their shared parent
directory and elaborates every module in a group against one combined library, matching how a
real Verilog-A toolchain treats a library folder handed to it together.

## The code

The AST's own arena discipline mirrors `va-ir`'s (`CLAUDE.md` §5): expressions are indexed by
`ExprRef`, not boxed or referenced directly, even at the surface-syntax level, before
elaboration ever runs:

```rust
pub struct ExprRef(pub u32);

pub enum ExprAst {
    Const(f64),
    Ident(String),
    Probe(Access),
    Unary(UnOp, ExprRef),
    Binary(BinOp, ExprRef, ExprRef),
    Call(String, Vec<ExprRef>),
    Ternary(ExprRef, ExprRef, ExprRef),
    ArrayLit(Vec<ExprRef>),   // `{a, b, c}` — never survives past elaboration as a value
    // …
}

pub enum Item {
    Direction { dir: Direction, nets: Vec<String> },
    Net { discipline: Discipline, nets: Vec<NetDecl> },   // incl. vector-net ranges
    Param { ty: ParamType, name: String, default: ExprRef, range: Option<Range> },
    Analog(Stmt),
    Function(AnalogFunction),
    Var { ty: ParamType, names: Vec<VarEntry> },          // incl. array-variable ranges
    Instance { .. },                                       // module instantiation
    // …
}
```

`Stmt` at this layer already carries the full analog control-flow set — `if/else`, `while`,
`for`, `repeat`, `case` — that `va-ir::Stmt` (Interface α) mirrors one level down after
elaboration resolves names.

## It works

```console
$ cargo test -p va-frontend parser
running 59 tests
test parser::tests::precedence_mul_binds_tighter_than_add ... ok
test parser::tests::pow_is_right_associative ... ok
test parser::tests::ternary_parses_and_is_right_associative ... ok
test parser::tests::bitwise_and_shift_precedence_and_parsing ... ok
test parser::tests::vector_net_declaration_parses ... ok
test parser::tests::two_d_vector_net_declaration_parses ... ok
test parser::tests::two_modules_in_one_file_both_parse ... ok
test parser::tests::zero_modules_is_not_an_error ... ok
test parser::tests::reserved_word_as_identifier_is_rejected ... ok
...
test result: ok. 59 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 157 filtered out; finished in 0.01s
```

Two of these closed real corpus gaps, not hypothetical ones: `zero_modules_is_not_an_error` —
a source file containing only `` `define ``s and no `module` at all (real corpus headers like
`generalMacrosAndDefines.va`) is a valid compilation unit per the LRM, not a parse error; and the
empty statement (a bare `;`) parses as a no-op, since `mvsg_cmc_3.2.0.va` uses exactly one as an
`if`'s entire body when its optional thermal port is left unconnected.

## Limitations

Stated in the parser module's own doc comment: errors are reported by token index, not source
byte offset (spans aren't carried past the lexer yet); parameter-range inclusive/exclusive
delimiters (`[0:inf)`) are recorded but dropped at elaboration; analog-function argument/local
*types* are parsed and discarded, direction only is kept; `@(event) stmt` parses but the trigger
is discarded (the controlled statement always runs — sound for a DC operating point, not for
real event scheduling); vector nets/array variables cap at 2 dimensions, a documented
(non-standard, for vector nets specifically) extension rather than general N-D support; a
`generate`-driven array of module instances doesn't parse yet.

## What's next

`t1-frontend/03-elaboration.qmd` — resolving this AST's names into `va-ir`'s arena IR, and the
much larger real-corpus coverage story that lives there.
